description:
A flight controls engineer at a regional aircraft OEM needs to define the control surface sub-surfaces on a master OpenVSP aircraft model so that stability analysis can begin. A specification file outlining the required control surfaces has been provided at /home/ga/Desktop/control_surface_spec.txt. 

Your task:
1. Open the specification file to read the required control surface parameters.
2. In OpenVSP, open the baseline model located at /home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/eCRM-001_wing_tail.vsp3.
3. Navigate to the Sub-Surface panel for the appropriate parent geometry components (Wing and Horiz).
4. Add three "Control Surface" sub-surfaces (Inboard Flap, Outboard Aileron, and Elevator) and configure their spanwise start and end parameters exactly as detailed in the specification.
5. Save the modified model as /home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/eCRM001_with_controls.vsp3.

Note: Make sure to attach the flap and aileron to the Wing component, and the elevator to the Horiz component. Use the "Control Surface" sub-surface type.
